title: Latent degradation state proxies
summary: Recent F1 degradation research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit stops as resets, not just static tyre age.
rationale: Recent F1 degradation research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit stops as resets, not just static tyre age. Current best features include degradation ratios but not an explicit recursively updated stint state.
implementation_hint: Within each Driver-Race-Stint ordered by LapNumber, compute causal EWMA and rolling slopes of LapTime_Delta, Pace_Deviation_From_Median, Cumulative_Degradation, and Position_Change. Add reset-aware features like ewma_pace_loss_since_stint_start, degradation_acceleration, and z-scored lap delta versus prior laps in same stint.
expected_effect: May identify tyres approaching the performance cliff before the model sees a large absolute degradation value.
risk: Sorting and lag construction must preserve row order and avoid future laps; noisy early-stint estimates may need min-period flags.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
